chapter_description: Coulomb's law, electric field, potential, capacitors, Gauss's law

questions.text: Two point charges of +3μC and −3μC are placed 20 cm apart. What is the electric field at the midpoint?
questions.explanation: E = kq/r² for each charge. At midpoint both fields point same direction, so add them. E = 2 × 9×10⁹ × 3×10⁻⁶ / (0.1)² = 5.4×10⁶ N/C

questions.text: Coulomb's law is analogous to which law in gravitation?
questions.explanation: Both follow inverse square law: F ∝ 1/r²

questions.text: A charge Q is distributed uniformly over a ring of radius R. What is the electric field at a distance x from the center along the axis?
Two charges of +4μC and −4μC are separated by 10 cm. Calculate the electric dipole moment (in C·m × 10⁻⁷).
A parallel plate capacitor has plates of area 100 cm² separated by 2 mm. If the dielectric constant is 5, find the capacitance in pF (picofarads). Round to 1 decimal.
